questions.question: Use “by” to name the agent: “The song was written {a1} Adele.”
Include a by-phrase when the agent is important: “The novel was written {a1} a Nobel Prize winner.”
Omit the by-phrase when the agent is obvious: “He {a1} yesterday.”
In “My car was stolen,” the agent is unknown, so the by-phrase is {a1}.
An organization can be an agent: “The decision was made {a1} the committee.”
Use “with” for an instrument: “The cake was cut {a1} a knife.”
Avoid weak agents: “English is spoken here,” not “English is spoken {a1} people here.”
If the agent is the focus, active may be clearer: “Shakespeare {a1} the play.”
Correct the instrument: “The door was opened {a1} a key.”
Use by for a natural force: “The tree was struck {a1} lightning.”
Both can appear: “The picture was painted {a1} Monet with oil paints.”
“The rules must be followed” is better than “The rules must be followed {a1} everyone.”
